claim: Hermes Agent's self-improving loop works best when users explicitly correct outputs, ask the agent to save learnings to memory, and create skills from repeated workflows—automatic learning without human guidance is insufficient
actionable: After each complex task, tell your agent: 'Save this to memory' or 'Turn this into a skill'; do not rely on automatic learning alone.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Agent memory architecture requires separating durable context (user.md, memory.md, soul.md) from session context; stateless startup means agents must reload all relevant context on every invocation
actionable: Maintain explicit memory files: user.md (preferences/style), memory.md (projects/business context), soul.md (personality), and agents.md (project scope); ensure these files are comprehensive enough to provide context without repetition across sessions.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Skills are reusable procedural playbooks with YAML front matter describing use cases; agents should not invoke skills without understanding their intended purpose—skill YAML clarity directly impacts invocation accuracy
actionable: When writing skills, write clear, specific YAML front matter describing when to use the skill; if an agent is not invoking a skill when it should, update the YAML description rather than the skill logic.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Cron jobs in Hermes must be self-contained and cannot recursively spawn more cron jobs; use context_from flag to chain outputs between sequential crons
actionable: When designing multi-step cron workflows, use context_from to pass output from job N to job N+1; do not attempt nested cron creation.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Docker containerization of agents isolates API keys and credentials per agent, enabling least-privilege access patterns and clear cost tracking when running multiple agents on a single VPS
actionable: Deploy each agent in its own Docker container with separate .env files; assign each agent only the API keys and scopes it actually needs (e.g., marketing agent doesn't need QuickBooks access).
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent via Telegram provides less visibility than CLI into session context window state and lacks full slash command support; CLI is better for deep coding work, Telegram for on-the-go task scheduling
actionable: Use Hermes CLI for complex iterative work (coding, debugging, architecture design); use Telegram interface for quick tasks, cron scheduling, and checks when mobile.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Stale or outdated memory files are the number-one cause of unexpected agent behavior and hallucinations; agents should regularly read and audit their own memory during sessions
actionable: If an agent is producing weird outputs, ask it to read and report its memory.md and user.md files; refresh memory by having the agent explicitly re-summarize and update these files.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Scale agent deployment incrementally; start with one main agent, migrate tasks to specialized agents only when they require different permissions, secrets, tools, memory scope, or audience
actionable: Use a decision tree: (1) Does this task need different permissions/secrets? (2) Does it need separate long-term memory? (3) Is it ongoing repeated work? Only create a new agent if yes to any question.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent installed on a VPS allows nightly automated backups to GitHub using built-in cron jobs; this backup pattern enables agent resurrection if VPS infrastructure fails
actionable: Immediately after Hermes setup, create a nightly cron job that commits your agent's state (skills, memory, configurations) to a private GitHub repo.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: API keys should never be pasted into agent chat windows; instead, set credentials in the agent's .env file via terminal (Hermes config set) to avoid logging secrets to conversation history
actionable: Never paste API keys or credentials into chat; always use 'hermes config set KEY value' or equivalent to store secrets in .env files outside conversation logs.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent context window threshold (136K tokens) triggers automatic memory compaction; failed compaction falls back to context markers, which may impact subsequent agent behavior if memory becomes stale
actionable: Monitor for context compaction messages in Hermes logs; if you see 'fallback context marker' warnings, manually refresh memory files and summarize key state before continuing long sessions.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent can be used in parallel with Claude Code to manage multi-agent infrastructure; Claude Code can diagnose VPS issues, manage credentials, and help scale agents without manual terminal commands
actionable: Maintain a Claude Code project that documents your Hermes VPS setup, container names, API key names, and security configurations; ask Claude Code to help with infrastructure debugging when Hermes agents fail.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent improves over time through the feedback loop: work → learning → memory/skill updates → future work; this loop requires deliberate user intervention and breaks without explicit correction and skill creation
actionable: Establish a post-task ritual: (1) Tell agent to save insights to memory, (2) Ask it to create or patch a skill from the work, (3) Give feedback on tone/approach in soul.md, (4) Review memory files weekly.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent via ChatGPT API (not native model) costs significantly less than dedicated API keys while providing access to the full model capability set (reasoning, vision, function calling)
actionable: If running Hermes Agent, use OpenAI ChatGPT subscription (20–200/month) instead of API-based billing for cost efficiency; still provides frontier models.
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)

claim: Hermes Agent differs from Open Claude primarily in stability, self-improvement architecture, and lighter footprint; Open Claude has larger team support but more frequent breaking updates
actionable: Evaluate Hermes Agent vs. Open Claude based on: (1) stability needs (Hermes more stable), (2) self-improvement importance (Hermes stronger), (3) community ecosystem (Open Claude larger).
source_title: Hermes Agent: Zero to Personal AI Assistant (1 Hour Course)
